description_long: This object is a lush, upright cluster of flowering rose stems, featuring multiple pale pink blossoms nestled among dense, vibrant green foliage. The flowers are large, with tightly layered petals forming classic, rounded rose shapes, and are distributed at varying heights throughout the plant. The leaves are abundant, ovate to lanceolate in shape, and arranged alternately along the slender, green stems. The blossoms are primarily located at the terminal ends of the stems, with some slightly off to the sides, giving the plant a natural, bushy appearance. The overall structure is airy, with visible gaps between leaves and flowers, and the stems are thin but sturdy. This type of flowering plant is commonly found in urban parks, gardens, or as decorative landscaping along sidewalks and building entrances.
description: A bushy cluster of pale pink roses with dense green leaves and slender stems, featuring multiple blossoms at the ends of the stems. The plant has a natural, airy structure and is typical for urban gardens or parks.
description_view_0: This view highlights the central cluster of pale pink roses, with the densest foliage and several blossoms clearly visible among the leaves.
description_view_1: From this angle, more blossoms are visible on the right side, and the foliage appears slightly less dense, revealing more of the stems.
description_view_2: This perspective shows the plant's left side, with a prominent rose in the foreground and leaves extending outward, giving a fuller view of the plant's width.
description_view_3: This view emphasizes the upper blossoms and the layered arrangement of leaves, with some flowers partially obscured by foliage.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged in a spiral, forming tightly layered, overlapping whorls typical of rose flowers, with a clear radial symmetry.
